heroSubtitle: AI-powered support for software delivery and digital transformation teams. Get instant answers on services, delivery models, and operational workflows.
howItWorksNote: Click the chat widget in the bottom right to try it out.

questions.title: Client Needs & Solution Fit
items: We’re planning a digital transformation initiative — how does bycoders structure a project from discovery to delivery?
Which of your core services (Data, Software Development, Team as a Service, UI/UX, Support & Maintenance) is usually the best starting point for a company like ours?
You mention tailor-made technological solutions — how do you customize architecture and product scope for each client?
How do you combine user experience design and engineering so that UX decisions are reflected in the final product?
As an official Shopify partner, what kind of fast, secure, and scalable e-commerce builds can you deliver, and for which business scenarios?

questions.title: Delivery Model, Team & Operations
items: How does your remote-first model (Brazil, Portugal, USA) work in practice for communication, handoffs, and timezone coverage?
In your Team as a Service model, what roles can you provide (e.g., software engineers, consultants, UI/UX), and how do you integrate with an in-house team?
What does ongoing Support & Maintenance include after go-live, and how do you handle issue prioritization and response times?
How do you ensure quality and performance while delivering high-impact software and digital products?
If we reach out through your contact form, what information should we provide to get the most accurate proposal, and what is your expected first-response time?

problems.title: Conversational CX Evaluation
problems.pain: Bycoders is in the evaluation stage for conversational customer support outcomes, with explicit decision criteria centered on ROI and cost. The opportunity needs a clear path from initial demo to validated business impact.
successCriteria: Validate CX Agent coverage for support-team workflows listed in the opportunity use cases.
Confirm that the evaluation produces decision-ready ROI and cost evidence.
Align the demo output with the current stage objective (3-evaluate) and next-step timeline.

problems.title: Enterprise Search Scope Definition
problems.pain: The opportunity includes Enterprise Search as a priority track, but the project needs explicit scope definition so search outcomes can be measured alongside conversational support outcomes during evaluation.
successCriteria: Define the minimum viable enterprise-search scope for the ongoing evaluation phase.
Map search outcomes to the listed support, operations, and customer-facing use cases.
Ensure search capabilities are assessed as a complement to CX Agent workflows.

problems.title: Cross-Function Use Case Alignment
problems.pain: The opportunity spans multiple teams and use-case tracks, which can create fragmentation during evaluation unless priorities, ownership, and rollout sequence are aligned around a single plan.
successCriteria: Prioritize the listed use cases into an agreed evaluation sequence.
Align stakeholder expectations across support, operations, and sales/CS workflows.
Keep the first phase focused on the highest-value outcomes for an SMB segment motion.

problems.title: Commercial Readiness for Close Plan
problems.pain: The opportunity has defined commercial parameters and a target close timeline, but the evaluation must translate into a concrete close plan that links validated scope to contract economics.
successCriteria: Tie evaluated scope to the current commercial model (ACV and ramped ARR).
Maintain momentum from evaluation to close-plan execution before target close.
Keep stakeholders aligned on term length and next-step cadence.
